description: ADR template tuned for LLM-serving systems (latency, cost, isolation).
context: Describe the LLM-serving use case: expected QPS, payload size, latency budget, privacy requirements, model class, deployment environment.
decision: State the serving topology, model choice, scaling strategy, and fallback posture.
consequences: Cover cost envelope, p50/p95 latency, capacity ceiling, operational overhead, security boundaries.
alternatives: Remote hosted API — simplest, but cost/privacy/cold-start.
Self-hosted GPU cluster — most flexible, but ops overhead.
CPU inference with quantized model — cheap, but quality/latency tradeoff.
risks: Model drift changes output shape and breaks downstream parsing.
